rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical iron-catalyzed azidoesterification of alkenes using carbazates as alkoxycarbonyl radical precursors and TMSN3 as the azide source to give beta-azido esters.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of PcFe(II) to PcFe(III), which oxidizes methyl carbazate to generate the methoxycarbonyl radical (with loss of N2); a second metal-azide complex (Fe or Mn) delivers N3 to the alkyl radical after C-C bond formation.

key_intermediates.name: beta-(alkoxycarbonyl) carbon-centered radical (I) after addition to alkene
M(III)-N3 azido metal complex (M = Fe or Mn)
M(II)-N3 complex after azide group transfer

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes PcFe(II) to PcFe(III), which oxidizes methyl carbazate to give methoxycarbonyl radical (releasing N2); this C-radical adds to the alkene to form a secondary C-radical that is trapped by an azido-M(III) complex via inner-sphere azide group transfer, regenerating M(II). Protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode.

probe_reagents.name: (1-phenyl-2-methylcyclopropyl)methylene alkene (radical clock 29)
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened product 30 obtained in 53% yield (E/Z = 2.5:1), consistent with a carbon-centered radical intermediate after addition.

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of TMSN3, methyl carbazate (1), PcFe, and MnBr2
probe_reagents.observation: Methyl carbazate oxidizes at 670 mV and TMSN3 at 340 mV; addition of PcFe to TMSN3 gives a new wave at ca. 160 mV (Fe(II)/Fe(III) with azide as ligand); this wave increases with added carbazate indicating redox communication between Fe(III) and 1. MnBr2 does not show analogous behavior.

probe_reagents.name: Controlled potential electrolysis at Eanode = 0.10 V vs Fc+/0
probe_reagents.observation: Azidoesterification of 4-tBu-styrene proceeded smoothly to give product 3 in 64% yield, confirming low-potential Fe-mediated oxidation is sufficient.

notes: Two metal catalysts are used cooperatively: PcFe is proposed as the redox-active mediator that oxidizes the carbazate to the alkoxycarbonyl radical, while MnBr2·4H2O (or PcMn in some cases) is invoked as the azide-delivery metal (M(III)-N3 -> M(II)). Mn alone is not catalytically competent (Table 1, entry 3). Cathode reaction is H2 evolution.
